channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: SEC fines Tennessee $100,000 for rushing the field after win over Alabama
item.description: While Tennessee was fined $100,000 when fans rushed the field after beating Alabama in 2022, this penalty is the school's first violation since the policy was revised last year.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, NewsNation

item.title: Patrick Mahomes and Chiefs stay undefeated, beat 49ers 28-18 in Super Bowl rematch
item.description: Patrick Mahomes overcame two interceptions to deliver a few big plays with his feet and the Kansas City Chiefs remained the league’s only undefeated team after beating the San Francisco 49ers 28-18 in a Super Bowl rematch.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dubow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Instant analysis of 49ers’ 28-18 loss to Chiefs in Super Bowl rematch
item.description: The 49ers lost 28-18 to the Kansas City Chiefs on Sunday, eight months after Kansas City repeated as the Super Bowl champion with an overtime win over the Niners.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Commanders lose Jayden Daniels to a rib injury in a 40-7 romp over the Panthers
item.description: The Washington Commanders blew out the Carolina Panthers 40-7 after losing rookie quarterback Jayden Daniels to a rib injury.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Browns QB Watson suffers likely season-ending Achilles injury. Teammates upset some fans cheered
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Deshaun Watson's tumultuous tenure with Cleveland has taken another significant turn. His third season appears to be over. The embattled quarterback was carted off the field Sunday with a right Achilles tendon injury he suffered on a noncontact play against the Cincinnati Bengals.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brandon McManus' field goal on the final play gives Packers a 24-22 victory over Texans
item.description: GREEN BAY, Wis. (AP) — Brandon McManus insisted he hadn't planned to do a “Lambeau leap” if he made a winning field goal in his Green Bay Packers debut. But when he kicked a 45-yard field goal as time expired to give Green Bay a 24-22 victory over Houston on Sunday, McManus couldn't help himself.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Everything is warmups for the New York Yankees until they get to the World Series
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — For the New York Yankees, the real opening day is World Series Game 1. A little bubbly for a playoff berth, the AL East, the Division Series and their 41st pennant were just warmups.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cooper scores in his Bills debut and Allen shakes off a sluggish start in 34-10 rout of Titans
item.description: Amari Cooper scored the go-ahead touchdown in his debut with Buffalo and Josh Allen overcame a sluggish first half to throw two touchdown passes in his 100th career start to help the Bills rally from a 10-point deficit and beat the Tennessee Titans 34-10.
item.dc:creator: John Wawrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ilia Malinin lands 4 quads — and a backflip — to win his third straight Skate America title
item.description: World champion Ilia Malinin won Skate America on Sunday for the third consecutive year, altering his free skate on the fly after an early mistake and punctuating the program with a backflip that had been banned in competition until this season. The two-time and reigning U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers-Chiefs pregame: Belichick, Gruden handicap game; Deebo ill
item.description: Former NFL coaches Bill Belichick and Jon Gruden see what cracks the 49ers' must fill to avoid another loss to the Chiefs today.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Bigsby scores 2 TDs as Jaguars show fight in 32-16 win over Patriots in London
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Jacksonville Jaguars didn't show any quit this week after falling behind early on Sunday against the New England Patriots. The Jaguars trailed 10-0 before rallying and overpowering the Patriots 32-16 at Wembley Stadium. “This is what our team can do,” coach Doug Pederson said.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Browns' Deshaun Watson carted off field with apparent Achilles injury
item.description: Cleveland Browns quarterback Deshaun Watson appeared to suffer a serious Achilles injury on Sunday against the Cincinnati Bengals and was carted off the field.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lynx and Liberty ready for winner-take-all Game 5 of WNBA Finals with history on line for both teams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The WNBA Finals have come down to a winner-take-all Game 5 on Sunday night with both New York and Minnesota looking to make history. The Liberty are looking for their first title having lost in the Finals five times.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Santa Monica Ferris wheel to light up in support of Dodgers World Series push
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers are knocking at the door of the World Series, and one of Los Angeles' most iconic landmarks will be decked out in blue to reflect the city's support.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Victor Wembanyama lived up to all the hype as an NBA rookie. In Year 2, the Spurs star wants more
item.description: Here are some of the awards that Victor Wembanyama received over the last 12 months: the NBA rookie of the year trophy, a spot on the All-Rookie team, a first-team All-Defense selection, three rookie of the month awards and most recently an Olympic silver medal. They’re all nice.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jets, embattled star Haason Reddick finally agree to deal that will end months-long holdout: report
item.description: The New York Jets and Haason Reddick have reportedly agreed to an "adjusted contract" that ends the nasty back-and-forth between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Juan Soto's 3-run homer in 10th sends Yankees past Guardians 5-2 and into World Series for 41st time
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Juan Soto was supposed to be the difference-maker, the one who pushed the New York Yankees back to the top. They're one step away.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Deion Sanders accuses Obama of 'playing political games' by picking Arizona to beat Colorado
item.description: Colorado head coach Deion Sanders called out Barack Obama after the former president incorrectly predicted Arizona would beat Sanders' Colorado Buffaloes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christian Barmore apologizes to Rhode Island police after racism remarks
item.description: Patriots defensive tackle Christian Barmore issued an apology on social media to the Providence Police Department after he accused officers of racism during a recent traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Raymond Baccari, NewsNation

item.title: No. 2 Oregon routs Purdue 35-0, remains on top of Big Ten with first road shutout in over 30 years
item.description: WEST LAFAYETTE, Ind. (AP) — Oregon coach Dan Lanning challenged his team to see how it would respond to a long road trip on a short week following its 32-31 victory over then-No. 2 Ohio State last week. The No. 2 Ducks aced that test Friday night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — With their season on the line, the Minnesota Lynx did just enough to stave off elimination and force a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals. Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Lynx beat the New York Liberty 82-80 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer, and the New York Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Real Salt Lake aims for a strong finish to regular season while honoring midfield hero Matt Crooks
item.description: Real Salt Lake (3-West, 56 points) closes out the regular season against Vancouver Whitecaps FC (8-West, 47 points) at America First Field in Sandy on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: These Dick's Sporting Goods stores will sell championship gear after closing hours if the Dodgers win on Friday
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers can punch their tickets to the World Series on Friday night, and Southern California fans itching to get their hands on some championship merchandise can do so quickly.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Former NFL quarterback Jay Cutler arrested on DUI, gun charges
item.description: The former Bears and Broncos quarterback Jwas arrested Thursday in Tennessee for allegedly driving intoxicated.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, Fox 5

item.title: Criticism sparked over Snapdragon Stadium field conditions
item.description: The San Diego Wave FC lost 1-0 to Mexico’s Club America Wednesday. Both teams are advancing to the semifinals, but it was the turf that was the talk of the tailgate.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Column: Caleb Williams has arrived and the Bears are a playoff team
item.description: Caleb Williams has shown impressive improvement in his first six games with the Chicago Bears, leading the team to three straight wins and a potential playoff berth.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
